CHANGE DIR
% cd Documents\STEM_ED_SIGNALS\testbook2_local

GITHUB
update files in github
git pull origin main
git add .
git commit -m "Update site content"
git push origin main

status checks:

git status
git log --oneline -5
git branch

A quick one-liner that often works when you've only changed content is:
git add . && git commit -m "Update website" && git push origin main

JUPYTERBOOK BEFORE PUSHING TO GITHUB




RUN JUPYTERBOOK LOCALLY


DEPLOY.YML BASE_URL
name: Jupyter Book (via myst) GitHub Pages Deploy
on:
  push:
    # Runs on pushes targeting the default branch
    branches: [main]
env:
  # `BASE_URL` determines, relative to the root of the domain, the URL that your site is served from.
  # E.g., if your site lives at `https://mydomain.org/myproject`, set `BASE_URL=/myproject`.
  # If, instead, your site lives at the root of the domain, at `https://mydomain.org`, set `BASE_URL=''`.
  BASE_URL: '/wiba.github.io'

VISIBILITY IPYNBcells ON GITHUBPAGES
remove-input #hides cell but shows output.
remove-cell #hides cell/input AND output
hide-input #hides cell but can be clicked open so code can be inspected
